title: The Interrupt System
summary: Covers interrupt types (hardware, trap, software), the interrupt controller, maskable vs non-maskable interrupts, and the complete interrupt handling sequence.
content_points: Interrupts allow the CPU to respond asynchronously to external events without polling.
Maskable: CPU can ignore them (IF=0); Non-maskable: always serviced.
Interrupt controller (Intel 8259): collects IRQ lines, arbitrates by priority, signals CPU.
Handling: save state on stack → clear IF → identify source (type byte) → look up vector table → jump to service routine → restore state → resume.
Vector table at address 0: 256 entries × 4 bytes = 1 KB.
Types: hardware (peripheral), trap (CPU-internal error), software (program requests OS service).
learning_objectives: Trace the complete interrupt handling sequence
Explain the interrupt vector table structure
Differentiate hardware interrupts, traps, and software interrupts
